name: Sherlock
access_notes: The core Sherlock project is a local command-line interface (CLI) tool and does not provide a public API for developers. Third-party integrations, such as the one on Apify, may offer APIs, but these are separate services not maintained by the Sherlock Project.
mcp_notes: The core project does not provide an MCP server. A third-party Apify wrapper for Sherlock exists that may support MCP, but this is not an official project offering.
blocker: The core project is a CLI tool and does not expose a public API; it is not designed for programmatic access.
auth_notes: The project does not have a public API, therefore it has no authentication methods.
one_liner: Sherlock is an open-source command-line tool designed to hunt down social media accounts by username across hundreds of social networks.
api_notes: The official project is a CLI tool. There is no official public API. The mentioned Apify integration is a third-party wrapper and not part of the Sherlock project. Another referenced product, CloudFerro's Sherlock, is a separate AI service unrelated to this project.

evidence.claim: Sherlock is primarily a CLI tool; it does not provide an official API.
A feature request for a REST API was closed by the maintainer, stating that a web API is not something they will be doing with the current version.
Apify provides a third-party wrapper (Actor) for Sherlock, which is distinct from the core open-source project.
